title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
After discovering that the victim, Andrew Tewell, was actually the one with nail holes in his palms and feet rather than the killer, Vain and Escham return to Arroyo. Vain contacts District Attorney Krümmit, who agrees to keep their unauthorized handling of the corpse a secret and arranges for Captain Pickert and a coroner to arrive. The pair then travels toward Stowberville, Ohio.

On Tuesday, a multi-state pursuit involving cars, trains, and planes begins. Ellery Queen arrives in Stowberville and receives a note from Professor Yadley at the Ford Stowberville Hotel. The note reveals that a man matching a specific description checked in at midnight and departed by taxi at 7:30 a.m. heading for Chinsville; the man had a bandage on his wrist and had abandoned a limp. Identifying this man as the killer, Ellery contacts the Chief of Police in Cheswick using the alias Ellery Quincannon to request assistance.

Ellery travels to the Clayton Inn in Cheswick, where Police Chief Hardy informs him that Yadley's contact reported the suspect changed his route to Columbus instead of Chinsville. At the Seneca Hotel in Columbus, Ellery finds another note from Yadley stating that the suspect, referred to as "K," took a 1:00 p.m. train to Indianapolis. Yadley intends to catch a plane to intercept him. After learning that a plane has already been rented for him at the airport, Ellery flies toward Indianapolis. Meanwhile, Vain and Escham arrive in Columbus and fly to Indianapolis via a plane arranged from Chillicothe. As they travel, Ellery reflects on the murder of Andrew Tewell, feeling confident in his progress.
